name: database-optimizer
description: Expert database optimizer specializing in modern performance
capabilities: Analyze query execution plans and optimize slow queries
Design advanced indexing strategies for high-traffic applications
Identify and resolve N+1 query problems in ORM code
Implement multi-tier caching architectures with Redis
Design database sharding and partitioning strategies
Optimize cloud database performance for AWS, Azure, and GCP
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected no suspicious patterns. This is a pure instructional prompt providing database optimization guidance. No scripts, network calls, filesystem access, environment access, or external commands are present. No prompt injection attempts detected.
valueStatement: Slow queries and poor database performance can cripple application responsiveness. This skill provides expert guidance on query optimization, indexing strategies, and scalable architectures to dramatically improve database performance.
limitations: Cannot directly execute database commands or modifications
Cannot access actual database systems or performance metrics
Cannot analyze specific query execution without actual query text
Recommendations may require database-specific expertise for implementation

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers working with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MongoDB
useCases.title: Application Developer Troubleshooting Slow Queries
useCases.description: Developer notices their application has slow page loads due to database queries. The skill provides step-by-step analysis of query patterns and optimization recommendations.

useCases.title: DBA Designing Indexing Strategy
useCases.description: Database administrator needs to design an indexing strategy for a high-traffic e-commerce application. The skill provides comprehensive guidance on index types and column ordering.

useCases.title: Architect Planning Database Scaling
useCases.description: Software architect planning to scale a monolithic application to handle increased load. The skill provides guidance on partitioning, sharding, and caching strategies.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Query Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me optimize this slow SQL query: [PASTE YOUR QUERY]. Analyze the query structure and suggest improvements for better performance.
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer has a specific slow query they want to optimize

promptTemplates.title: Indexing Strategy Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an indexing strategy for our [DATABASE TYPE] database that handles [DESCRIBE YOUR WORKLOAD - e.g., high read, frequent updates]. Our main query patterns include [LIST QUERY PATTERNS].
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to design or improve database indexes for an application

promptTemplates.title: N+1 Query Resolution
promptTemplates.prompt: We are experiencing N+1 query problems in our [ORM NAME] application when loading [DESCRIBE ENTITIES]. The current code does [DESCRIBE CURRENT PATTERN]. How can we optimize this?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer dealing with N+1 query issues in their ORM

promptTemplates.title: Database Scaling Architecture
promptTemplates.prompt: Our database is experiencing [DESCRIBE PROBLEM - e.g., high write volume, slow queries under load]. Current setup: [DESCRIBE CURRENT ARCHITECTURE]. What scaling and optimization strategies would you recommend?
promptTemplates.scenario: Planning database architecture changes for scalability

bestPractices: Always measure before optimizing - use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to understand actual query performance rather than guessing
Design indexes based on actual query patterns, not theoretical knowledge - profile your application's most frequent queries
Implement comprehensive monitoring with slow query logs and performance baselines to detect regressions early
antiPatterns: Indexing every column 'just in case' - this increases write overhead and storage without proportional benefit
Ignoring N+1 queries in ORMs - always use eager loading, batch queries, or DataLoader patterns
Applying generic optimization advice without analyzing your specific workload patterns and data distribution

faq.question: What databases does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle, MongoDB, DynamoDB, and cloud databases including RDS, Aurora, Azure SQL, Cloud SQL, and DynamoDB.

faq.question: Can this skill execute database commands for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and recommendations. You must execute any database commands yourself in your own environment.

faq.question: How do I know which indexes to create?
faq.answer: Analyze your slow query log and most frequent queries. Create composite indexes matching your WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses. Use covering indexes for read-heavy workloads.

faq.question: What is the best caching strategy for my application?
faq.answer: Use a multi-tier approach: L1 in-memory for hot data, L2 Redis for distributed caching. Choose cache-aside for reads, write-through for data that must be consistent.

faq.question: How do I handle database scaling for high traffic?
faq.answer: Start with read replicas and connection pooling. Then consider partitioning (horizontal/vertical), sharding, or moving to a distributed database like CockroachDB or Aurora.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It provides optimization guidance that works with any AI assistant helping with database tasks.
